_comment: Ground-truth labels for real-world face photos in `image tests people/`.
These are the 'production distribution' — normal selfies/portraits.
Every image in this file is a manually reviewed adult face with clearly
visible skin. Labels reflect what a human dermatology reviewer would say.
acne         — expected acne severity: clear | mild | moderate | severe
skin_type    — expected type: dry | oily | normal | combination
skin_issues  — a list of ALL acceptable predictions. If the person's face
has no visible issue, use ['healthy']. If there is some
minor imperfection, list every plausible interpretation.
Add new images here as you collect them — the tests will pick them up
automatically. If unsure about a label, leave the field as null and the
test will skip that assertion for that image.

woman_pic_1.jpg.notes: Clear, matte, well-lit portrait. No visible blemishes.

images (2).jpg.notes: Mid-adult woman, clean skin. Minor natural lines around eyes only.

images (4).jpg.notes: Young adult male, clear skin, no visible acne.

images (5).jpg.notes: Young adult male, clear skin, no visible acne.
